name: MemoryBankMCP
observations: FastMCP-based MCP server exposing 14 tools, 5 resources, 1 prompt
Entry point: main.py:main() calls mcp.run()
Global singleton engine=MemoryBankEngine() instantiated at module load
Dependencies: mcp[cli]==1.10.1, fastmcp==2.10.2, Python>=3.10
Project root stored in .aim/ only when .aim exists; otherwise storage is project-local only

name: MemoryBankEngine
observations: Owns DatabaseAdapter instance (self.db_adapter) and in-memory patterns dict (self.patterns)
Constructor auto-detects project root by walking CWD upward for .git/package.json/requirements.txt/Cargo.toml/go.mod/composer.json
Constructor calls MigrationManager.auto_migrate_if_needed() on every init - triggers JSON-to-SQLite migration silently if memory_storage.json exists and DB is empty
Loads active session ID from DB at init; self.current_session starts as None if no active session
patterns dict starts EMPTY at every startup - DB patterns table is NOT restored to in-memory dict on init
No .sessions or .memories dict attributes - export tools referencing engine.sessions/engine.memories will raise AttributeError

name: DatabaseAdapter
observations: All SQLite I/O is here - opens a new connection per method call (no connection pooling)
db_path = project_root/memory.db; creates parent dirs on init
Checks only for sessions table existence to decide whether to run schema.sql - partial schemas not detected
_get_current_session_id() raises ValueError if no active session - called by add_memory; error propagates unhandled through MCP tool layer
set_active_session deactivates ALL sessions then activates one - only one active session globally
migrate_from_json is a stub that returns True without doing anything
Collections store member IDs twice: in memories_json blob column AND via memories.collection_id FK - can desync

name: MigrationManager
observations: One-way migration from memory_storage.json to SQLite
auto_migrate_if_needed: only runs if JSON exists AND SQLite has zero sessions/memories
_perform_migration inserts sessions, then memories, then collections without FK validation
After migration creates timestamped backup: memory_storage.backup_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json
migrate_from_json stub in DatabaseAdapter is separate from and unrelated to MigrationManager

name: schema.sql
observations: 9 tables: sessions, memories, collections, patterns, package_apis, codebase_patterns, coding_sessions, validation_checks
4 UPDATE triggers auto-update last_updated/last_detected/last_used/updated_at timestamps
sessions.is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1 - new sessions are active by default but set_active_session is always called on create
memories.collection_id FK to collections with ON DELETE SET NULL
memories.revision_of FK to memories(id) with ON DELETE SET NULL - revised-away memories stay in DB forever

name: models.py
observations: 7 pure dataclasses: Memory, Collection, MemorySession, PackageAPI, CodebasePattern, CodingSession, ValidationCheck
No validation logic - invalid field values pass through silently
Memory.tags and CodebasePattern.tags default to [] via __post_init__
ValidationCheck.suggestions defaults to [] via __post_init__
MemorySession.main_thread is List[str] of memory IDs; MemorySession.collections is Dict[str, Collection]

name: create_memory_session
observations: Parameters: problem, success_criteria, constraints (comma-separated str), session_type (default=general)
session_type in [coding_session, debugging_session, architecture_session] also inserts a coding_sessions row
Session ID is first 8 chars of UUID4 - collision possible but unlikely
Always calls set_active_session which deactivates all prior sessions

name: store_memory
observations: Parameters: content, dependencies (comma-separated IDs), confidence, collection_id, code_snippet, language, pattern_type
If code_snippet provided, also calls store_codebase_pattern with pattern_type or memory_code
memory number is computed as len(main_thread)+1 or len(collection.memories)+1 at call time - NOT atomic, race-safe only in single-threaded MCP context
Calls _detect_contradictions which does keyword polarity matching against dependency memories
Returns WARNING prefix if contradictions detected

name: revise_memory
observations: Creates a NEW Memory record with revision_of=original_id; does NOT delete or mark original
Replaces original_id in main_thread or collection.memories by list index
Raises ValueError if original_id not found in list (no soft fallback)
Original memory stays in DB orphaned if revision replaces its reference

name: export_session_to_file
observations: BROKEN: references engine.sessions[engine.current_session] at line 613 - MemoryBankEngine has no .sessions attribute after SQLite refactor
Will always raise AttributeError at runtime
Supports markdown and json format parameters

name: export_memories_to_file
observations: BROKEN: references engine.sessions[engine.current_session] at line 710 and engine.memories[memory_id] - MemoryBankEngine has neither attribute
Will always raise AttributeError at runtime

name: discover_packages
observations: Iterates sys.modules at call time - only sees modules already imported in the running process
Calls importlib.import_module and inspect.getmembers - can trigger side effects in modules
Caps output at 10 API entries per module via _extract_api_info return slice
Silently skips modules that raise any exception during introspection

name: validate_package_usage
observations: Uses ast.parse on code_snippet - any syntax error sets result=failed
Regex patterns for reinvention detection are coarse (e.g., def.*request.*http)
Writes ValidationCheck to DB regardless of result
Returns warning not error on potential reinvention

name: load_codebase_context
observations: Walks project_path (default: os.getcwd()) looking for .py/.js/.ts/.java/.cpp/.c files
For each .py file stores first 1000 chars as a codebase_pattern of type structure
Can generate many DB rows on large projects with no row limit
File read errors are silently continued

name: get_memory_tree
observations: URI: memory://tree
Returns JSON with problem, main_thread list, and collections dict
Each memory node includes: id, content, number, confidence, bool(contradictions), revision_of, dependencies
Does NOT include tags or timestamps in tree output

name: get_analysis
observations: URI: memory://analysis
patterns_detected field reads from engine.patterns in-memory dict - always empty after server restart
_assess_quality uses only len(session.main_thread) and len(session.collections) thresholds: <3=insufficient, <7=basic, collections>0=advanced, else=good

name: ProjectRootDetection
observations: _detect_project_root walks CWD and all parents looking for: .git, package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, go.mod, composer.json
First match wins - stops at shallowest matching ancestor
Falls back to CWD if no marker found
Running MCP server from /home/user vs /home/user/myproject produces different DB paths silently
